title: How to get hired by Hunkin Law LLC — the AI-edge personal-brand pitch
html:
How to get hired by Hunkin Law LLC — the AI-edge personal-brand pitch

Source: 2025-01-03 Apple Note. Yahir's interview-prep talking points for Hunkin Law LLC — a Grants Pass-area legal practice founded by Joshua Hinkin (typo: "Hunkin"). Written 3 weeks after leaving China in the post-trip job-search bridge period.

TL;DR

  • 5-bullet pitch for the interview:
    1. Confident but not boastful.
    2. Grants Pass background — has volunteered at direct community levels (local credibility).
    3. Data-driven, computer science background.
    4. Can use traditional techniques but is significantly ahead of the curve in leveraging AI tools for social media — multilingual workflows, AI captions, long-content-to-shorter-engaging-videos.
    5. Read founder Joshua Hinkin's book in interview prep.
  • The job is for social-media / marketing role at a small-town legal practice — Yahir positioning his AI/CS skills as the unique edge against more conventional candidates.
  • This is the earliest captured instance of Yahir explicitly framing AI-leverage as career differentiator — the same instinct that becomes Rankett in late 2025 (small businesses get found via AI).

Pattern observed

The Hunkin Law pitch is proto-Rankett at small-business-services scale: Yahir realizes that small operators in Grants Pass are not yet using AI for marketing, and he can be the bridge. He doesn't take this job (no further mention in corpus), but the insight compounds — the same realization eventually scales into Rankett's "your SEO team handles the rest, we handle the AI search layer."

Also: reading the founder's book before the interview = same diligence-discipline as the Jensen Q&A prep and the AGM startup advice file.

Open questions

  • Did Yahir get the Hunkin Law interview / job? Probably not — no follow-up notes.
  • What was Joshua Hinkin's book?
  • Was this only seen as a bridge job, or was there genuine interest?
